title: React JS Basics Tutorial for Beginners | JSX, Vite, Components Explained
curriculum_role: Fast project prerequisite: orient the learner to Vite, JSX, functional components, props, and basic component composition before starting the app.
added_topics: Vite app setup and development server | JSX | Functional components | Props | Basic component hierarchy
decision_reason: It explicitly names Vite, JSX, and components, which are all learner gaps and prerequisites for following the project tutorial. Its 160-second duration keeps the setup lightweight, although the depth is uncertain.

title: Build a Complete To-Do List App with React + Vite | Dashboard, Reminders & Local Storage Tutorial
curriculum_role: Primary project spine: build the small CRUD-style React app and connect the foundational concepts to forms, state, filtering, and persistence.
added_topics: Habit-like item creation, editing, completion, and deletion | useState-driven event handling | Controlled form submission | Filtering | useEffect and localStorage synchronization | Integrated React/Vite project workflow
decision_reason: This is the closest available match to the requested weekend project: a React/Vite to-do app with local storage. The assessment attributes coverage of the major state, form, persistence, and filtering requirements, while noting that metadata cannot verify the implementation details.

title: Every React Concept Explained in 12 Minutes
decision_reason: A broad 12-minute concept explainer is likely theory-heavy and potentially superficial, conflicting with the learner's preference for project-based content. Its metadata does not verify Vite, forms, hooks practice, or persistence.

title: Learn React Hooks: useState - Simply Explained!
decision_reason: Its useState focus is relevant, but the selected project tutorial is assessed as covering the required state and event-handling work in context. This standalone explanation is less project-oriented and does not add forms, components, or persistence.

title: Handling Complex Multiple Input Form States in React [2 LINES ONLY]
decision_reason: Complex multiple-input form state is narrower and more advanced than the needs of the small app. It does not indicate coverage of the surrounding React project, CRUD state, or local persistence.

title: Learn the useState Hook for React
decision_reason: It is another narrow useState introduction. The selected project spine already supplies useState in the context of the requested app, making this additional short explanation unnecessary.

title: Persist State to localStorage in React (Complete Tutorial)
decision_reason: It focuses on localStorage persistence, which the selected project tutorial is already assessed as covering. It does not clearly add the missing CRUD, forms, filtering, or component work, so its marginal contribution is insufficient.

title: Custom React Local Storage Hook
decision_reason: A custom localStorage hook may introduce an unnecessary advanced abstraction for a first React weekend project. It also lacks evidence of covering the app's setup, components, forms, and CRUD workflow.

title: Save State to LocalStorage & Persist on Refresh with React.js
decision_reason: It provides another localStorage persistence treatment that overlaps with the selected project's assessed persistence coverage. Metadata does not establish a distinct useEffect or synchronization depth that would justify adding it.
